item.text: Reading `package.json` only and leaving the workspace unchanged.
Both agents are running. I’ll wait for their summaries and relay the useful parts.

`package.json` only:

- Package name: `effect-codex-app-server`
- Metadata: `private: true`, `type: module`
- Exports: `./client`, `./schema`, `./rpc`, `./protocol`, `./replay`, `./errors`, all pointing to TypeScript source files under `src/`
- Scripts:
  - `dev`: tsdown watch build for selected entrypoints
  - `build`: tsdown ESM/CJS/DTS build
  - `prepare`: `effect-language-service patch`
  - `typecheck`: `tsc --noEmit`
  - `test`: `vitest run`
  - `generate`: runs `scripts/generate.ts`
  - `probe`: runs Codex app-server probe example
- Dependencies: only `effect`
- Dev dependencies: Effect tooling, OpenAPI generator, platform-node, Vitest integration, `tsdown`, `typescript`, `vitest`
- Versioning uses workspace catalog protocol: `catalog:`

item.text: Reading `tsconfig.json` only and will report the requested structure.

`tsconfig.json` structure:

- `extends`: `../../tsconfig.base.json`
- `compilerOptions.plugins`: uses `@effect/language-service`
  - `namespaceImportPackages`: `["@effect/platform-node"]`
  - diagnostics:
    - `importFromBarrel`: `error`
    - `anyUnknownInErrorContext`: `warning`
    - `instanceOfSchema`: `warning`
    - `deterministicKeys`: `warning`
- `include`: `["src", "scripts", "test"]`
- `exclude`: not specified

Notable choice: this package relies mostly on the shared base config, with package-specific Effect language service diagnostics layered on top.

item.text:
Done. I spawned two explorer subagents:

- `package.json`: reported package metadata, exports, scripts, and dependencies.
- `tsconfig.json`: reported base config inheritance, Effect language service plugin diagnostics, includes, and lack of explicit excludes.
